fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Texas Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Texas - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity texas, how to transfer dietitian license to texas, dietitian compact texas, texas dietitian endorsement application

board.name: Dietitians Program
board.parent_dept: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR)

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian (LD)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice dietetics in Texas. Requires current CDR registration and successful completion of the Texas Jurisprudence Examination.

steps.title: Pass the Texas Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Before applying, you must take and pass the online Texas Jurisprudence Exam covering state laws. You will pay a $34 fee to the third-party provider.

steps.title: Apply Online via TDLR
steps.description: Create an account in the TDLR Online Licensing System. Submit your application for a new Dietitian License and pay the $108 fee.

steps.title: Pass the DPS Background Check
steps.description: TDLR conducts a criminal background check through the Texas Department of Public Safety (DPS). You will be sent a 'FAST Pass' to complete fingerprinting.

documents.item: Copy of current CDR registration card

provisional_text: Texas no longer offers a provisional license. The TDLR formally removed the provisional license designation from state law.
reciprocity_text: Texas utilizes licensure by equivalence. If you hold a current CDR registration, you have met the core educational requirements. Out-of-state applicants apply for a new license, submit their active CDR card, pass the Texas Jurisprudence Exam, and undergo the background check.

faqs.question: How much does a Texas dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial license application fee is $108, paid to TDLR. You must also pay a $34 fee to a third-party vendor to take the mandatory Texas Jurisprudence Examination, bringing the total to $142.

faqs.question: What is the Texas Jurisprudence Exam for Dietitians?
faqs.answer: It is a mandatory, online examination covering state laws, rules, and regulations relating to the practice of dietetics in Texas. All initial applicants must pass it.

faqs.question: Does Texas have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Texas. To work in Texas, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Texas?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Texas while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.

reciprocity.notes: Texas utilizes licensure by equivalence. If you hold a current CDR registration, you have met the core educational requirements. Out-of-state applicants apply for a new license, submit their active CDR card, pass the Texas Jurisprudence Exam, and undergo the background check.
